behavior_summary: Provides the 'U8P_POTSPANS::look' (examine) response for the POTSPANS actor: when the player examines this actor the script emits a single short bark containing the text "pot ".
The process appears to yield/suspend immediately after emitting the bark, ending the U8P_POTSPANS::look handling.
No global flags are read or written, no parameters or local variables are used, and there are no external function calls in the visible code.
notable_control_flow: U8P_POTSPANS::look is a process-oriented handler triggered by the game's examine/U8P_POTSPANS::look action.
Control flow is linear and trivial: emit one Bark ('pot ') then suspend/terminate; there are no branches, loops, or calls.
Presence of low-confidence 'Unknown' nodes in the decompilation suggests a suspend/yield step surrounding the bark but no additional behavior.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text ('pot ') intentional or an artifact/truncation from decompilation?
Does a higher-level engine mechanism perform any follow-up (e.g., logging or voice playback) after the suspend that isn't visible in this snippet?
Are there any adjacent/hidden handlers (not present in this file) that rely on POTSPANS being examined (no flags are set here, but other scripts could check external state)?
